codeReview.summary: I reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `1a40c32`, and the resulting editor top-bar implementation. The feature does satisfy the structural and accessibility parts of the contract: `EditorView.tsx` renders `BrandMark` from `@/ui`, the required file action buttons, Settings, a `Go Live` CTA with an accessible name, and the dirty indicator now exposes `aria-label="unsaved changes"` when dirty. However, the feature still misses a stated styling requirement: the top-bar UI is not design-token-only, because the relevant header CSS continues to hardcode multiple `rgba(...)` color values instead of using token variables.

issues.description: The Go Live CTA styling still hardcodes color values in the top-bar redesign. `.gp-btn-live` and its related selectors use raw `rgba(...)` literals for the border, glow, hover border, and box shadows (for example lines 516, 548, 556-561, and 573-574) instead of token variables. That violates the feature contract in `features.json` (`All elements use design tokens, no hardcoded colors`) and the mission styling guidance that frontend visuals should use design-token CSS variables.

The file indicator portion of the redesigned header also keeps hardcoded colors. `.gp-filecrest`, `.gp-filecrest:hover`, `.gp-filecrest__dot`, and `.gp-filecrest__dot--dirty` use raw `rgba(...)` values for background, border, and glow/shadow styling (for example lines 2010-2017, 2025-2026, and 2034-2043) rather than design-token variables. Because the feature explicitly requires the header to use design tokens with no hardcoded colors, this is a contract miss.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-redesign` skill and the worker handoff disagree with the actual procedure used here. The skill says workers should invoke `frontend-design` at the start and do a RED-first test cycle, but this session's transcript skeleton shows only `mission-worker-base` and `frontend-redesign`, and the first `npm run test:frontend` run was a green baseline before edits. The handoff still marked `followedProcedure: true`, so either the skill is too strict for this kind of feature or the handoff reporting rubric is overstating adherence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\skills\frontend-redesign\SKILL.md:21 requires invoking `frontend-design` at session start, and lines 41-45 require writing tests first and running a failing RED-phase frontend test. The transcript skeleton at C:\Users\mitte\.factory\missions\fe650437-0d94-4ece-8085-ac6da736d0ec\worker-transcripts.jsonl:10 shows `Skill mission-worker-base`, later `Skill frontend-redesign`, a green baseline `npm run test:frontend`, then edits; the handoff file C:\Users\mitte\.factory\missions\fe650437-0d94-4ece-8085-ac6da736d0ec\handoffs\2026-04-10T14-03-00-611Z__editor-topbar-redesign__23399e40-ffe6-416a-96b4-2f5b4ba0ff74.json reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail. The feature's accessibility and structure updates are in place, but the redesigned editor top bar still uses hardcoded RGBA colors in `src/index.css`, so it does not satisfy the design-token-only styling requirement.
